authoritative_sources.title: Power Platform Well-Architected
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Microsoft Learn is the official vendor source for Power Platform architecture guidance, including reliability, security, operational excellence, and performance considerations for low-code apps and automations.

authoritative_sources.title: Application lifecycle management with Microsoft Power Platform
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Microsoft Learn is the official source for Power Platform ALM, solutions, component packaging, and environment movement used as delivery and evidence artifacts.

authoritative_sources.title: Data policies
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Microsoft Learn is the official source for Power Platform connector data policies that constrain connector grouping, blocked connectors, and tenant-governed data movement.

authoritative_sources.title: Handle errors in cloud flows
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Microsoft Learn is the official Power Automate source for cloud-flow error handling patterns, run-after behavior, and diagnostics that shape workflow failure handling.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: failure handling, remediation, and resubmission tracking

authoritative_sources.title: Secure use reference for GitHub Actions
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GitHub Docs is the official source for GitHub Actions secure workflow usage, including secret, token permission, untrusted input, and third-party action risk.

authoritative_sources.title: Workflow syntax for GitHub Actions
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GitHub Docs is the official reference for workflow files, events, jobs, permissions, and execution semantics used when reviewing GitHub Actions automation artifacts.

authoritative_sources.title: Error handling
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: n8n Docs is the official source for n8n workflow-level error handling and error workflow behavior used in troubleshooting and exception routing.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: failure handling, remediation, and resubmission tracking

authoritative_sources.title: Security audit
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: n8n Docs is the official source for n8n security audit checks across credentials, nodes, workflows, and instance settings.

authoritative_sources.title: Business Process Model and Notation, Version 2.0.2
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: OMG BPMN is a recognized public workflow modeling standard for process, event, activity, gateway, and choreography terminology.

authoritative_sources.title: Secure Software Development Framework (SSDF) Version 1.1
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST SP 800-218 is an official public secure software development framework for integrating secure development practices into software and automation delivery.

authoritative_sources.title: OWASP Citizen Development Top 10
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: OWASP is a recognized public application security community, and its citizen development project identifies risk classes specific to low-code/no-code, business innovation platforms, connectors, data exposure, and automation governance.
used_for: connector and data exposure review criteria

authoritative_sources.title: OWASP API Security Top 10 2023
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: OWASP API Security Top 10 is a recognized public baseline for API authorization, authentication, rate limiting, and validation risks that apply to webhook and connector automations.

authoritative_sources.title: RFC 9110: HTTP Semantics
authoritative_sources.source_class: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IETF RFC 9110 defines HTTP method, status, header, and semantics used by workflow connectors, webhooks, retries, error handling, and API integration review.

authoritative_sources.title: RFC 9457: Problem Details for HTTP APIs
authoritative_sources.source_class: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IETF RFC 9457 defines a standard structure for HTTP API error details, supporting workflow troubleshooting, evidence capture, and consistent error communication.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: failure handling, remediation, and resubmission tracking

authoritative_sources.title: ACM Code of Ethics and Professional Conduct
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: ACM is a recognized professional computing association, and its code establishes confidentiality, competence, and harm-avoidance principles relevant to client delivery and professional authority boundaries.

workflow_stages.name: scope and authority intake
workflow_stages.description: Confirm automation goal, business owner, tenant context, platform candidates, systems affected, data classification, client confidentiality terms, approval matrix, and adjacent ownership before making design or review claims.
source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: evidence collection and system inventory
workflow_stages.description: Collect flow exports, workflow files, solution packages, process diagrams, connector inventory, API contracts, credential model, run history, job logs, policy records, test results, and approval artifacts.
source_classes: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: workflow design and implementation assessment
workflow_stages.description: Assess trigger/action decomposition, process state, platform fit, integration dependencies, idempotency, duplicate suppression, retries, compensation, rate limits, data mapping, portability, and rollback design.
source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks
Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: security, governance, and maintainability review
workflow_stages.description: Review connector governance, DLP constraints, OAuth scopes, credential handling, webhook validation, least privilege, naming, modularity, branch complexity, source control, and confidential delivery limits.
source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks

workflow_stages.name: failure handling, remediation, and resubmission tracking
workflow_stages.description: Classify failures, preserve diagnostic evidence, recommend retry or compensation handling, identify owners, define validation criteria, and prepare remediation or resubmission notes.
source_classes: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: delivery packaging and approval-aware closure
workflow_stages.description: Prepare review summaries, evidence checklists, assumptions, blockers, approval dependencies, rollback points, monitoring expectations, no-regression evidence, and client-safe delivery notes without claiming final signoff.
source_classes: Client-delivery, documentation, or engagement workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: handoff, refusal, or low-confidence escalation
workflow_stages.description: Return to orchestrator when missing evidence, conflicting policy, confidentiality constraints, client rights, professional judgment, legal or contractual authority, or adjacent platform ownership controls the answer.
source_classes: Professional standards or recognized public frameworks

artifacts: automation request, business outcome, success criteria, workflow owner, approval matrix, client confidentiality terms, and tenant policy context
Power Automate flow export, Power Apps component inventory, Power Platform solution package, environment plan, DLP policy record, connector inventory, and flow run history
GitHub Actions workflow file, workflow run logs, permissions, environments, secrets and variables policy, branch protection, deployment records, and job failure output
n8n workflow definition, execution history, error workflow, credential/project record, source control comparison, and security audit output
BPMN or process diagram, trigger/action decomposition, event schema, API contract, webhook specification, HTTP status/error mapping, rate-limit documentation, and idempotency design
test matrix, validation evidence, smoke test result, rollback plan, runbook, monitoring/logging expectations, and no-regression evidence
review or inspection summary, evidence checklist, maintainability findings, remediation note, resubmission checklist, blocked-readiness note, and orchestrator handoff package
systems_of_record: Power Platform admin center, maker/admin portals, environment inventory, DLP policy records, solution history, connector inventory, and flow run history
GitHub repository, Actions workflow runs, deployment environments, secrets and variables policy, branch protection, issue tracker, and release records
n8n workflow store, project/credential records, execution history, error workflows, source control integration, and security audit output
API gateway, webhook provider, API specification repository, schema registry, identity provider application records, and integration error logs
source control system, issue tracker, change request system, test management records, release tracker, evidence repository, rollback runbook, and operational knowledge base
tenant policy repository, security baseline, risk register, exception register, confidentiality record, approved recipient list, client contract reference store, and approval matrix

allowed: classify low-code and workflow automation requests as in-scope, out-of-scope, or blocked on missing evidence or authority

recommend workflow architecture, platform/component fit, process decomposition, connector usage patterns, idempotency, retries, compensation, testing, observability, rollback, and maintainability remediation
prepare implementation and hardening checklists, review summaries, evidence checklists, troubleshooting guidance, remediation notes, and client-safe delivery packages
map vendor documentation, public standards, tenant policy, and observed workflow evidence to advisory recommendations with explicit assumptions
route adjacent execution, approval, legal, contractual, risk, and professional-judgment decisions to the orchestrator or human reviewer
escalate: required workflow exports, run history, API contracts, credential model, tests, approval records, confidentiality rules, or tenant policies are missing or contradictory
vendor guidance, public standards, tenant policy, client contract, regulated obligation, or jurisdictional rules materially conflict
client rights, professional judgment, legal interpretation, contractual approval, audit attestation, executive risk acceptance, procurement, licensing, or budget authority controls the answer
remediation requires platform operations, identity administration, infrastructure configuration, monitoring execution, incident response, compliance policy ownership, or product/commercial decisions
refuse: perform cloud platform operations, deep infrastructure hardening, site reliability execution, network configuration, firewall/DNS changes, monitoring platform administration, or incident command
administer identity platforms, execute IAM policy, rotate credentials, access secrets, or perform tenant-wide access changes
implement unrelated core framework code, custom backend services, database schema ownership, or full-stack features outside workflow automation scope
provide legal advice, procurement decisions, contractual approval, audit attestation, regulator signoff, executive portfolio planning, pricing, product roadmap, or editorial approval
approve production release, waive evidence, suppress findings, disclose confidential client data to unapproved recipients, fabricate evidence, or claim final engineering/legal/contractual signoff

domain_failure_modes: workflow recommendations are made without flow exports, workflow files, run history, API contracts, connector inventory, credential model, tests, or approval records

a low-code platform is recommended because it is convenient even though error handling, volume, rate limits, ALM, tenant policy, or security evidence indicate custom engineering or adjacent handoff is required
connector or webhook designs omit authentication, anti-replay, schema validation, least privilege, DLP policy checks, credential rotation, or confidential payload handling
idempotency, duplicate suppression, retries, compensation, dead-letter routing, rollback, and resubmission criteria are absent from mutable or externally-triggered workflows
run logs or client records are shared outside approved recipients, or secrets and tokens are repeated in review output
the specialist substitutes for legal, contractual, audit, executive risk, procurement, identity, infrastructure, SRE, product, or pricing authority because the work is automation-adjacent
tenant-specific adaptation weakens the public baseline, mixes tenants, or records confidential tenant policy and payload data into shared catalog artifacts
a connector or workflow engine update is treated as a harmless patch without schema review, regression evidence, test updates, and no-regression approval
conflicting public guidance, vendor documentation, tenant policy, or client contract is confidently synthesized without authorized source precedence

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Missing evidence or workpaper support
Client request that crosses into advisory or signoff authority
Confidentiality or approval conflict in delivery workflow
normal implementation/design work
maintainability and correctness review
edge cases and failure modes
security and quality expectations where applicable
out-of-scope rejection
low-confidence escalation
no unreviewed regressions
Scenario proving output fidelity for: Workflow architecture recommendations
Scenario proving output fidelity for: Implementation and hardening checklist
Scenario proving output fidelity for: Maintainability and correctness review notes
Scenario proving output fidelity for: Troubleshooting and escalation guidance
Boundary case proving refusal: Cloud platform operations, deep infrastructure hardening, and site reliability execution.
Boundary case proving refusal: Legal procurement, executive portfolio planning, or commercial pricing strategy.
Boundary case proving refusal: Pure identity platform administration, network configuration, and IAM policy execution.
Boundary case proving refusal: Core framework coding implementation outside workflow automation scope.
Escalation case proving orchestrator return: Return when confidence is low or evidence is insufficient.
Escalation case proving orchestrator return: Return when request is primarily platform operations, compliance policy ownership, or commercialization scope.
Evidence bundle is incomplete or inconsistent.
Finding requires remediation before closure.
Request for definitive signoff outside lane authority.
Scenario proving client or content workflow handling with evidence and approval awareness.
Scenario proving escalation when confidentiality, professional judgment, or contractual authority changes the action.
Scenario proving the lane refuses or escalates when: do not imply legal, editorial, or contractual approval authority without explicit scope.
Scenario proving the lane refuses or escalates when: escalate when client rights or professional judgment determine the answer.
Normal in-scope execution using realistic industry terminology and artifact shapes.
Boundary rejection when the request should move to an adjacent specialist.
Low-confidence handling when required facts, records, or authority sources are missing.
Conflicting-source or conflicting-policy handling that requires escalation instead of confident synthesis.
Tenant-specific adaptation handling that preserves the public baseline and records assumptions.
